channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: President Trump calls to delay nomination of intel pick Jay Clayton
item.description: The Senate was set to begin the confirmation process for Jay Clayton as Director of National Intelligence Today. But President Trump is calling for a delay.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate was set to begin the confirmation process for Jay Clayton as Director of National Intelligence Today. But President Trump is calling for a delay.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Trump to face questions at G7 press conference. And, Tuesday's primary results
item.description: Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.
item.content:encoded:
Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.

(Image credit: Ludovix Marin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'Rejected': How federal prisons stonewall grievances and deny care for years
item.description: People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.
item.content:encoded:
People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.

(Image credit: Michelle Mildenberg Lara for The Marshall Project)
item.dc:creator: Christie Thompson

item.title: 'Coreano Hermano': Ahead of Mexico vs. South Korea, it's all love between the fans
item.description: It's an unusual sight before a World Cup match: Supporters of opposing teams partying together, calling each other "brother," some even hoping for a draw. But fans say they're far from rivals.
item.content:encoded:
It's an unusual sight before a World Cup match: Supporters of opposing teams partying together, calling each other "brother," some even hoping for a draw. But fans say they're far from rivals.

(Image credit: Karla Gachet for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: FAQ: How World Cup fans and workers can stay safe in the heat
item.description: More than 1 in 3 World Cup matches face dangerously hot, humid weather. Here's how to protect yourself from heat illness.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1 in 3 World Cup matches face dangerously hot, humid weather. Here's how to protect yourself from heat illness.

(Image credit: Lynne Sladky)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Soccer's premier goal scorers, led by Lionel Messi, show up to the World Cup with a bang
item.description: The World Cup might be less than a week old, but the goal scoring at this year's tourney has been sublime. Argentina's Lionel Messi netted three goals the same day France's Kylian Mbappé scored two.
item.content:encoded:
The World Cup might be less than a week old, but the goal scoring at this year's tourney has been sublime. Argentina's Lionel Messi netted three goals the same day France's Kylian Mbappé scored two.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: With Iran deal, Trump told ships to 'start your engines.' That's not happening yet
item.description: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz inflicted global pain during the months-long conflict with the U.S. and Israel. A tentative deal is in place, but questions remain about the key waterway.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz inflicted global pain during the months-long conflict with the U.S. and Israel. A tentative deal is in place, but questions remain about the key waterway.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Trump's Iran agreement dominates G7 but big questions remain
item.description: The U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran has rocked the global economy and decimated Trump's standing at home
item.content:encoded:
The U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran has rocked the global economy and decimated Trump's standing at home

(Image credit: Isabel Infantes)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: On a flight home, a stranger helped her understand what came next
item.description: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.content:encoded: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kwerel

item.title: El Niño is here, so what does it mean?
item.description: An El Niño has formed amid the warmer-than-normal waters in the tropical Pacific. Now it's a question of how intense the phenomenon will be and where effects like heat and drought will strike.
item.content:encoded:
An El Niño has formed amid the warmer-than-normal waters in the tropical Pacific. Now it's a question of how intense the phenomenon will be and where effects like heat and drought will strike.

(Image credit: Jhojan Hilarion)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell
